# validate.breakpoint_flicker { #tslai.validate.breakpoint_flicker }

```python
tslai.validate.breakpoint_flicker(tracks, sample, state=0)
```

Posterior discontinuity across a sample's segment boundaries (CLAUDE.md §7.3).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**tracks**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[Segment](`tslai.output.Segment`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Posterior segment tracks per sample.

<code>[**sample**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sample id whose consecutive-segment boundaries are examined.

<code>[**state**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Ancestry-state index whose posterior probability is compared.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Keys ``"mean_abs_diff"`` (mean ``\|P_left(state) - P_right(state)\|`` over boundaries), ``"flip_rate"`` (fraction of boundaries where the ``argmax`` state changes) and ``"n_boundaries"`` (number of segment boundaries).
